input.question: We are a student engineering team designing a vehicle for the FSAE competition. Attached is the FSAE rules document. What does rule T.2.7.2 state exactly? Answer with only the text of the rule and no other words.

retrieved_context:
RULE: t.2.7.2
            TITLE: the anti-submarine belts of a 6 point harness must be mounted in one of the following ways:

            CONTENT:
            rule T.2.7.2: The Anti-Submarine Belts of a 6 point harness must be mounted in one of the following ways:
T.2.7.2 The Anti-Submarine Belts of a 6 point harness must be mounted in one of the following ways: 
a. With the belts going vertically down from the groin, or with an Anti-Submarine Belt Side 
View Angle up to 20° rearwards.  The Anti-Submarine Belt Mount Spacing should be 
approximately 100 mm apart.  
 
b. With the Anti-Submarine Belt Mounting Points on the Primary Structure at or near the 
Lap Belt anchorages, the driver sitting on the Anti-Submarine Belts, and the belts coming 
up around the groin to the release buckle.

final_answer: rule T.2.7.2: The Anti-Submarine Belts of a 6 point harness must be mounted in one of the following ways: T.2.7.2 The Anti-Submarine Belts of a 6 point harness must be mounted in one of the following ways: a. With the belts going vertically down from the groin, or with an Anti-Submarine Belt Side View Angle up to 20° rearwards. The Anti-Submarine Belt Mount Spacing should be approximately 100 mm apart. b. With the Anti-Submarine Belt Mounting Points on the Primary Structure at or near the Lap Belt anchorages, the driver sitting on the Anti-Submarine Belts, and the belts coming up around the groin to the release buckle.
